data.content:
The build generates `GeneratedInfo.cs` successfully but never adds it to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn never sees it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` after writing it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file before `CoreCompile`, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild collects the `Compile` items before `CoreCompile` runs, so the generated file is never passed to the compiler.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the `GenerateSampleCode` target using `<ItemGroup>` with `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />`.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to MSBuild's `<Compile>` item group. The compiler only compiles files it knows about through that item group.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation after being written.
